channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: BMI Says Outlook for Oil Prices in Q3 Now Highly Uncertain
item.description: The outlook for oil prices in the third quarter of 2026 is now highly uncertain, analysts at BMI warned.

item.title: Oil prices climb 2% as Middle East hostilities escalate
item.description: Oil prices climbed by approximately 2% on Wednesday, 15 July, as rising hostilities between the US and Iran heightened concerns.

item.title: Sunda Energy applies for offshore Taranaki Basin exploration permit
item.description: Sunda Energy submitted an application for a petroleum exploration permit (PEP) in the offshore region of the Taranaki Basin in New Zealand.

item.title: IMO SecGen Says Hormuz Is Too Dangerous for Ships
item.description: The International Maritime Organization said the Strait of Hormuz remains too dangerous for commercial vessels to transit.

item.title: Ukraine Says It Hit Russian Oil, Gas Tankers in Black Sea
item.description: Ukrainian forces hit multiple Russian vessels in the Black Sea overnight.

item.title: LNG Canada offers equity option to First Nations for phase two tank
item.description: LNG Canada gives equity option to MNT Investments LP that could allow a First Nations partnership to invest up to C$1bn ($711m).

item.title: Ukraine Intensifies Attacks on Russian Tankers in the Black Sea
item.description: After striking 116 vessels linked to Russia’s shadow fleet in the Sea of Azov in recent weeks, Ukraine’s military is turning its sights on ships in the Black Sea, hitting in drone attacks as many as 20 vessels overnight…

item.title: Oil Prices Climb for Fourth Day as Iran Threatens New Energy Chokepoints
item.description: Oil prices rose early on Wednesday for the fourth consecutive trading session as the collapsed U.S.-Iran ceasefire precipitated a new crisis in the Strait of Hormuz and Iran threatened to close “all other export…

item.title: Brent Futures Flip to Backwardation as Middle East Supply Risks Return
item.description: The Brent Crude futures curve flipped into backwardation this week, signaling expectations of tight prompt supply for the first time in a month, as the market started pricing in the renewed hostilities in the Middle…

item.title: U.S. Gasoline Prices Could Hit $4 Per Gallon Within Days
item.description: Following a few weeks of reprieve for drivers, the U.S. national average price of gasoline could top $4 per gallon within a week, as crude oil prices rallied by about 12% in the three days since Friday amid the…

item.title: U.S. Backs Iraq-Syria Oil Pipeline to Bypass the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States supports plans by Iraq and Syria to rebuild a damaged oil pipeline that would carry crude from Kirkuk to Syria’s Mediterranean coast and bypass the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. backs the Iraqi and Syrian…

item.title: China’s Refinery Runs Crash to Pandemic Lows as Crude Imports Collapse
item.description: Chinese refiners further slashed crude processing in June, with volumes crumbling to the pandemic lows of 2020 amid Strait of Hormuz supply disruptions and weakening domestic fuel demand. China’s refinery throughput…

item.title: Oil Hits One-Month High
item.description: Oil climbed to a one-month high as renewed Middle East fighting heightened concerns over supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: The Next Oil Rally May Depend On China, Not The Middle East
item.description: Chinese refiners largely stopped competing for Middle Eastern crude during the Iran conflict, leaving more Gulf cargoes available to Europe, India, and the rest of Asia just as traders prepared for a supply shock. The…

item.title: Bangladesh's Nuclear Power Play Is a Test for Emerging Economies
item.description: Previously, we reported that India is betting big on solar energy to power its industrialization drive, abandoning earlier plans to rely on coal. Indeed, India is going off the beaten path by attempting to become the…

item.title: Canada Ties New West Coast Pipeline to Oil Sands Expansion
item.description: Canada’s biggest oil sands producers, the Alberta provincial government, and the federal government have reached a new milestone in advancing the planned new West Coast pipeline that would move another 1 million barrels…

item.title: US Crude Oil, Gasoline Inventories Still Falling
item.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 564,000 barrels in the week ending July 10. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 399,000 barrels…

item.title: Could Geothermal Energy Rescue Germany's Fading Coal Towns?
item.description: Europe’s largest economy is walking a precarious tightrope toward carbon neutrality, trying to balance energy security with climate goals as this summer’s heat waves create mounting pressure on the continent’s energy…

item.title: Trump’s Hormuz Toll Could Upend Global Energy Trade
item.description: A proposed 20% charge on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz would violate the basic principles of international navigation, raise energy costs and undermine the stated purpose of restoring stability. It could…

item.title: Greenfire to acquire Canadian oil sands producer Connacher for $900m
item.description: Greenfire Resources has agreed to acquire Canada-based Connacher Oil and Gas in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $900.4m.
